sentence_reviews.text: Provenance and citation tracking attach claims to source identifiers, retrieved passages, transformations, timestamps, and evidence paths.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA and RAG sources support evidence retrieval and grounding; provenance metadata details are only broadly supported.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim is defensible, but direct provenance/citation-tracking evidence would be stronger.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Provenance, citations & evidence tracking is to connect External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including Neural search, RAG & question answering, Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation, Content credentials, signing & provenance.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support several underlying concepts, but the downstream capability list is atlas-derived rather than directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Treat as a graph-positioning claim; dependencies are plausible but the full downstream list needs direct source support.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The stale/private/contradictory/incorrectly ranked limitation is generally plausible for memory and retrieval systems, but most packets cite only broad background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation should be narrowed or backed by a memory/retrieval safety source; dependency list portion is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Provenance, citations & evidence tracking uses External retrieval & ranking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.

sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.
